title: Explainability of Text Processing and Retrieval Methods: A Survey
abstract: This paper provides an organized summary of major research efforts in explainability in textual information processing and retrieval. It aims to complement existing surveys by discussing more recent work in areas already covered and focusing on areas not addressed by previous surveys.
key_points: Historically, text processing used interpretable features like words, phrases, counts, morphology, parts of speech, and grammatical structures
Recent advances in Deep Machine Learning have revolutionized text processing but made models less interpretable
Text is now represented as dense vectors in high-dimensional spaces
There's growing research interest in explainability of these complex models
Several surveys already exist in this area, and this paper aims to complement them
